mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates persistent inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures, allowing monetary policy to stabilize and supporting positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 4.0% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal broad consumer demand contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above the 2017 baseline indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which boosts short-term equity momentum during supply-driven recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal reverses direction when tested against periods of supply chain bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% establishes a high discount rate environment that systematically depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in real GDP growth reflect expanding economic activity that directly lifts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 290, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing aggressive rate hikes that compress equity multiples and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal fails to maintain predictive power when CPIAUCSL remains above 290 for extended periods.
